addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

## devops-mcp
This DevOps MCP intentionally exposes a small always-on MCP tool surface:
health, list_capabilities, get_capability_details, tool_search, and invoke_tool.

For every host, Docker, systemd, file, or audit-log task, call tool_search first
to find the right hidden operation. Then call invoke_tool with the exact operation
name and an args object matching the operation description. Do not assume direct
tools such as run_command, docker_ps, read_file, or service_status are listed in
tools/list; they are discoverable operations to keep client context small.

Call list_capabilities to browse by category or safety class. Call
get_capability_details for one operation's full contract, including args and
examples. Call health first when you need server context, visible tools,
registered agents, or the complete operation-name list. Treat write-capable
operations with care: this server has root-level host access, so prefer
inspection commands before state-changing commands.

## recall-ai
Recall.ai lets developers send meeting bots to video calls, capture meeting data, and connect bots to calendar events. This MCP server provides read-only tools scoped to a Recall.ai workspace. A workspace is the Recall.ai account context that bots, calendars, docs defaults, and logs are read from. By default, the MCP uses the workspace selected when the user authorized the connection. If the account has access to multiple workspaces, workspace-scoped tools accept an optional workspace_id so the agent can read from a different workspace for that call. Use get_info to see the current workspace and API version. When the user names a workspace, call list_workspaces, match the name from the returned list, then pass the returned workspace ID to bot, calendar, log, and docs tools. Do not pass a workspace name as workspace_id. Use bot tools to list bots, inspect bot details and lifecycle events, and page through bot logs when debugging join, recording, or meeting behavior. Use list_calendars/get_calendar to inspect connected and disconnected calendar integrations, platform emails, credential status, and calendar connection/sync state. Use calendar event tools to see which bots were scheduled from calendar events. Use log tools for workspace-level API, calendar connection, recording, transcript, webhook, and integration errors when a bot-specific log query is too narrow. Use list_webhook_endpoints and list_webhook_deliveries when debugging dashboard webhook subscriptions, missing delivery attempts, destination responses, or a resource-specific webhook event such as a bot, calendar, recording, transcript, or artifact event. For usage, consumption, or billing-volume questions (hours or minutes consumed, usage by month/day, usage by platform or by customer metadata, plan limits, usage spikes, top-consuming bots, recording or transcription or storage volume), use the usage tools instead of adding up list_bots results: get_usage_summary for aggregates and plan context, list_bot_usage for per-bot rows such as longest sessions o… [truncated]

## synology-monitor
This NAS MCP intentionally exposes only a small always-on tool surface:
list_capabilities, get_capability_details, tool_search, invoke_tool, run_command,
check_disk_space, and restart_nas_api.

For any NAS diagnostic, troubleshooting, recovery, storage, Drive/ShareSync,
backup, package, log, filesystem, snapshot, permission, or admin task, call
tool_search first unless the task is exactly disk-space checking, free-form
read-only shell diagnosis, or restarting nas-api. Most NAS capabilities are
hidden from tools/list by design to keep the AI session context small.

list_capabilities browses by group and safety class without invoking anything.
get_capability_details returns one operation's full contract. tool_search returns
clear operation descriptions plus the exact invoke_tool call shape. After choosing
a capability, call invoke_tool with the exact returned name, target, and args. Use
target "edgesynology1", "edgesynology2", or "both".

Write tools require a preview first. To execute after reviewing the preview,
call invoke_tool again with confirmed: true inside args. Do not invent or expose
bearer tokens.

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx or .potx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx or .potx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates (.potx), layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx or .potx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). 1	# AGENTS.md — popcrm-web
2	
3	Canonical operating guide and documentation router for **popcrm-web**. Read this
4	first; load other docs only when the task needs them (see the Documentation map).
5	
6	## Project summary
7	
8	`popcrm-web` is the **POP CRM frontend** — a Vite/React/TypeScript single-page app
9	served by nginx, used by internal POP Creations staff to run customer, sales,
10	licensing and product-development workflows: customers, contacts, the program
11	pipeline, Outlook-ingested email routing, Fireflies meeting notes, notes, tasks,
12	licensor approvals, and AI-model settings.
13	
14	It **stores no data of its own** — every read/write goes through the shared
15	Supabase project (`https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`). The CRM backend is
16	a Supabase/Postgres port of the earlier Twenty→Directus CRM data model. The
17	outcome that matters: a fast, dense operations console over that shared data.
18	
19	- Production: `https://crm.designflow.app`
20	- Preview alias: `https://crm-dev.designflow.app`
21	- Backend (Supabase): `https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`
22	- Fireflies webhook/health: `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app`
23	- Sibling frontends (separate repos): `poppim-web` (PIM), `popdam-web` (DAM)
24	
25	## Multi-model AI note
26	
27	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
28	
29	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
30	
31	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
32	
33	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
34	
35	Always start with:
36	
37	- `AGENTS.md`
38	
39	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
40	
41	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/`, `frontend_imp.md` |
44	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow/shape changes | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
45	| Add/change config, env vars, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
46	| Change local setup, dev/test/lint scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, `package.json`, `eslint.config.js` | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
47	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release, or rollback | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf` | Local-only dev docs |
48	| Change data shape, Supabase fields, queries, views, RPCs, or identifiers | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/database.types.ts`, `src/features/crm/api.ts`, `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` | Deployment docs unless rollout changes |
49	| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md` (Critical incidents), area-relevant source, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Unrelated docs |
50	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md` (if present) and the docs it names | Docs outside the handoff scope |
51	| Understand the redesign rationale (charts, tokens, layout) | `frontend_imp.md` (historical plan — fully implemented) | Everything else |
52	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless the task requires them |
53	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/`, ignore files | Source files except to verify accuracy |
54	| Pull secrets from 1Password (MCP server or `op` CLI) | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/1password.md` | Unrelated architecture/deploy docs |
55	
56	Notes:
57	- `HANDOFF.md` is **absent** when there is no unfinished work. If it exists, it is
58	  required reading for continuation tasks.
59	- `frontend_imp.md` is a large background design plan — now fully implemented. It is in
60	  `.claudeignore`/`.cursorignore` to keep it out of routine AI context.
61	- `design_handoff_popcrm_elevation/` is a historical design-spec directory — also ignored.
62	- Shared non-code infrastructure/server standards live in
63	  [`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
64	  When deployment, domains, runtime ownership, server dependencies, break-glass
65	  runbooks, or infrastructure decisions change for this app, update that repo too.
66	
67	## Shared-backend startup/shutdown hygiene
68	
69	Why this exists:
70	`popcrm-web`, `poppim-web`, and `popdam3` all depend on the same Supabase backend.
71	An unfinished migration or dirty canonical `u2giants/shared-db` checkout can block
72	unrelated app commits or, worse, ship a database change without the right preview
73	checks. Future AI sessions must keep shared-db work isolated and leave the
74	workspace clean enough for the next vibe-coding session.
75	
76	Startup checklist:
77	
78	1. Run `git status --short` in this repo before editing.
79	2. If the task may touch Supabase schema, RLS, API views/RPCs, generated database
80	   types, or cross-app data contracts, also run `git status --short` in
81	   `/worksp/shared-db` before editing.
82	3. Treat `shared-db/` inside this repo as a read-only mirror. Do not create or
83	   edit migrations there; use canonical `/worksp/shared-db`.
84	4. If `/worksp/shared-db` has untracked migrations or unrelated dirty files, stop
85	   and report them before creating new database work. Do not mix another
86	   session's shared-db changes into this app's commit.
87	5. Before creating a shared-db migration, create/switch to a dedicated
88	   `/worksp/shared-db` branch named for the database change. App repos commit to
89	   `main`; shared-db uses branch + PR.
90	
91	Shutdown checklist:
92	
93	1. Run `git status --short` in this repo and, if touched or inspected for backend
94	   work, in `/worksp/shared-db`.
95	2. No untracked shared-db migration may remain. Every shared-db migration must be
96	   committed on its own branch, stashed with a clear name, or removed if
97	   abandoned.
98	3. If shared-db work is incomplete, leave durable handoff text that names the
99	   branch/stash, migration file, preview/prod apply status, and the next exact
100	   action.
101	4. Final reports must separate app commits from shared-db status so the owner can
102	   keep vibe-coding without becoming the git janitor.
103	
104	## Repository structure
105	
106	Project-owned application code:
107	
108	- `src/app/` — shell + router: `AppLayout.tsx`, `routes.tsx`, `navigation.ts`
109	- `src/components/app/` — shared building blocks: `DataTable`, `DetailDrawer`,
110	  `MetricCard`, `PageToolbar`, `AppPage`, `AppSidebar`, `AppHeader`, `Combobox`,
111	  `CommandSearch`, `FilterSelect`, `StatusBadge`, `states.tsx`
112	- `src/features/crm/` — CRM domain:
113	  - `CrmDataContext.tsx` — loads all collections once; exposes state, refresh, stats
114	  - `api.ts` — Supabase view/RPC/table reads and writes · `constants.ts` · `format.ts` · `useRecordSelection.ts`
115	  - `pages/` — one module per route (Overview, Pipeline, Customers, Contacts,
116	    EmailRouting, Meetings, Notes, Tasks, Approvals, Settings) + `_shared.ts`
117	  - `components/` — domain drawers (Email/Opportunity/Customer/Contact/Task/Note/
118	    Meeting/Approval) + `CrmStatusBadge`, `RelationLabel`
119	- `src/auth/auth.tsx` — Supabase auth/profile state · `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`
120	- `src/lib/` — `supabase.ts` (client), `database.types.ts` (generated schema), `types.ts` (frontend domain types), `utils.ts`
121	- `src/App.tsx`, `src/main.tsx`, `src/index.css` (shared OKLCH design tokens)
122

243	## Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
244	
245	### Bootstrap loader tolerates a failing collection
246	
247	Looks like:
248	`CrmDataContext.load()` uses `Promise.allSettled` and per-collection setters instead of one `Promise.all`.
249	
250	Actually:
251	Each Supabase view/table/RPC load runs independently; a failing one leaves its
252	section empty and a hard error shows only if everything fails.
253	
254	Why:
255	A single 403 (e.g. a Directus permission/schema gap on one collection) previously blanked the entire app. See Critical incidents 2026-06-12.
256	
257	Do not change because:
258	Reverting to all-or-nothing makes one bad collection take down every page.
259	
260	### Commit stamp comes from build args, not git, in CI
261	
262	Looks like:
263	`vite.config.ts` reads `process.env.COMMIT_HASH` / `COMMIT_DATE` before falling back to `git`.
264	
265	Actually:
266	The Docker build context excludes `.git` (`.dockerignore`), so git is unavailable inside the image build; CI passes the values as Docker build args.
267	
268	Why:
269	The app header shows the deployed commit + NYC time; without build args the stamp would be empty in CI builds.
270	
271	Do not change because:
272	Removing the env path silently blanks the build stamp in production.
273	
274	### `crm_licensor_approval_thread.stage` is free-form
275	
276	Looks like:
277	There's no `APPROVAL_STATUSES` enum; tone/labels are keyword-matched (`approvalTone`, `isApprovalResolved` in `constants.ts`).
278	
279	Actually:
280	The CRM approval `stage` field has no fixed choices, and the collection currently
281	has 0 rows.
282	
283	Why:
284	The real schema differs from earlier assumptions; matching keywords is robust to unknown values.
285	
286	Do not change because:
287	Hard-coding an enum would re-introduce the wrong field/value assumptions that caused the 2026-06-12 incident. Verify real values via the backend once approval data exists.
288	
289	### `customer_status` labels/tones are context-specific — `OTHER` is NOT a global label override
290	
291	Looks like:
292	You could add `OTHER: 'Not a Customer'` to `format.ts` `LABEL_OVERRIDES` to relabel it.
293	
294	Actually:
295	`OTHER` is a shared enum value — it also means "Other" for `chain_type`, `contact_type`,
296	and routing. The customer-status relabel/recolor lives in
297	`constants.ts:customerStatusLabel` / `customerStatusTone` (and `CUSTOMER_STATUS_LABEL`),
298	used only by the Customers table/drawer — never in the global `label()`.
299	
300	Why:
301	A global `OTHER` override would wrongly rename every other column's "Other".
302	
303	Do not change because:
304	Putting `OTHER` (or `UNASSIGNED`) into the global `label()` overrides silently
305	corrupts chain/contact/routing labels. Status colors: green active, yellow
306	potential, blue New Company (untriaged), gray Not-a-Customer — no red.
307	
308	### Customers Triage is ingested domains, not customer rows
309	
310	Looks like:
311	You could load Customers → Triage from `api.crm_customer_list` rows where
312	`customer_status` is `UNASSIGNED`.
313	
314	Actually:
315	Email-domain noise lives in `crm.ingested_domain` and is exposed through
316	`api.crm_ingested_domain_list`. Those rows are not customers and should
317	use the `CrmIngestedDomain` frontend type, not `Retailer`. The Triage table shows
318	domain evidence and a promote action; it must not render customer-only inline
319	status/chain edits or `CustomerDrawer`.
320	
321	Why:
322	`core.customer` is the shared hub used by CRM, PM, DAM, and PLM. Random email
323	domains must not become shared customer rows until a human promotes them with
324	`crm.promote_ingested_domain(...)`, which creates a potential customer
325	(`is_potential = true`).
326	
327	Future sessions should:
328	Keep customer lists and customer pickers on customer-scoped API contracts
329	(`api.customer_list` for shared/basic reads, or a CRM-specific
330	`api.crm_customer_*` view when CRM-owned fields are needed); keep Customers
331	Triage on `api.crm_ingested_domain_list`. Do not add new `account`-named API
332	contracts or callers. Existing `api.crm_account_list` / `api.crm_update_account`
333	objects are legacy compatibility names and should only be dropped after every
334	deployed client has moved to customer-named contracts. If a frontend change requires a new
335	view/RPC/policy/trigger, make that change in canonical `/worksp/shared-db` and
336	document it in the appropriate `u2giants/shared-db` `.md` file, not this repo's
337	vendored `shared-db/` mirror.

339	### Customer tabs use server segments
340	
341	What changed:
342	Browser verification on 2026-06-28 showed broad
343	`api.crm_customer_list?select=*` reads and exact counts could still time out
344	after the account-to-customer contract rename. Migration
345	`20260629033000_crm_customer_segment_timeout_fixes.sql` adds
346	`api.crm_customer_segment_list(p_segment, p_limit)` and
347	`api.crm_customer_segment_counts()`.
348	
349	Why:
350	The Customers page needs active, dismissed, and all counts/rows, but the browser
351	should not page or count the full customer compatibility view. Segment RPCs
352	filter `core.customer` directly and return the needed rows/counts quickly.
353	
354	Future sessions should:
355	Keep Customers page tabs and active customer pickers on
356	`useCustomerSegmentQuery` / `useCustomerSegmentCountsQuery`. Do not restore
357	full browser paging or exact head counts against `api.crm_customer_list`.
358	
359	### Contacts page customer segmentation and customer edit choices
360	
361	What changed:
362	Contacts are customer-facing only when their linked customer has
363	`customer_status` `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER` or `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER`. `Cust Contacts`
364	requires that customer and no department; `Dept. Contacts` requires that
365	customer and a department; all contacts not linked to a customer
366	belong in `Triage`.
367	
368	Why:
369	Contacts linked to reviewed non-customers (`OTHER`) or untriaged customers were
370	previously appearing in customer/dept sections, which overstated the customer
371	contact list.
372	
373	Future sessions should:
374	Keep `ContactsPage` customer dropdowns row-aware. Customer/dept rows should offer
375	only Active/Potential customers; triage rows may offer Active/Potential
376	plus `OTHER` ("Not a Customer") customers so users can classify contacts without
377	showing every customer in the system.
378	
379	Likewise the Department dropdown is row-aware (`departmentOptionsFor`): it filters
380	`crm_department` by the row's selected customer (`idOf(d.retailer) === accountId`),
381	matching the pattern in `EmailDrawer`. With no customer selected it falls back to
382	all departments. Changing a row's customer also clears any department that no
383	longer belongs to the new customer (handled in `editCell`). Previously the
384	Department column used a single static list of every department regardless of the
385	chosen customer.

387	### Contact relationship edits require customer context and explicit clear flags
388	
389	What changed:
390	On 2026-06-23, the Contacts page and Data Admin contact cleanup were updated to
391	send the current customer (`retailer`) whenever editing relationship-owned fields
392	such as `department`, `contact_type`, or `scope`. Migration
393	`20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql` updates
394	`api.crm_update_contact` with explicit `p_clear_*` flags so the UI can clear
395	customer, department, type, and scope values intentionally.
396	
397	Why:
398	`core.contact_company.company_id` is required, so department/type/scope edits
399	must target a specific contact-company relationship row. The previous RPC used
400	`coalesce`, which made `null` mean "leave unchanged"; Data Admin's "Move and
401	Clear Type" and inline unassign actions could appear to work optimistically while
402	the database preserved the old value.
403	
404	Future sessions should:
405	Do not remove the frontend fallback to the old RPC shape until the shared-db
406	migration is confirmed applied in production. When editing relationship-owned
407	contact fields, include the contact's current customer (or infer it from the
408	selected department) and use explicit clear flags rather than assuming `null`
409	will clear a backend value.

411	### Contacts load by Supabase server segment; avoid derived-field filters/order
412	
413	What changed:
414	After the Supabase cutover, Contacts read through browser-safe API views. On
415	2026-06-22, `shared-db` migration
416	`20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql` preserved `api.crm_contact_list`, added
417	the explicit `app.has_app_access('crm')` guard, and introduced
418	`api.crm_contact_segment_list` / `api.crm_contact_segment_counts`. The Contacts
419	page now fetches Cust Contacts, Dept. Contacts, and Triage as server-computed
420	segments; All is lazy-loaded only when opened.
421	
422	Why:
423	The original `security_invoker` view plus PostgREST ordering/filtering on derived
424	fields (`name`, `company_customer_status`) timed out during the paged browser
425	load (page `2000-2999`), so `CrmDataContext` set contacts empty and the UI showed
426	all contact counts as 0 even though 8,654 contacts existed. Later, trying to cap
427	contact lists client-side hid most records. Server segments give each visible tab
428	complete data without forcing the All list to load immediately.
429	
430	Future sessions should:
431	Do not re-add server-side `order('name')` or
432	`.in('company_customer_status', ...)` to contact fetches. Use
433	`crm_contact_segment_list.crm_segment = customer|department|triage` plus
434	`crm_contact_segment_counts`; keep table search/sort client-side inside the
435	loaded segment. `api.crm_contact_list` remains the generic/fallback contract. If
436	Contacts are zero, test the exact authenticated REST pages and check
437	`app.profile.auth_user_id` / `app.app_access` before assuming data is missing.
438	
439	### Email Routing uses a recent feed plus server counts
440	
441	What changed:
442	After the customer-contract rename, browser verification showed Email Routing
443	could still time out because the app paged the full joined
444	`api.crm_email_routing_queue` view and computed segment counts client-side.
445	Migration `20260629031500_crm_timeout_fixes.sql` adds
446	`api.crm_email_routing_recent(p_limit)` and
447	`api.crm_email_routing_segment_counts()`.
448	
449	Why:
450	Production has enough historical emails that a broad joined queue read can hit
451	PostgREST statement timeouts and leave the UI empty or stale. The recent feed
452	limits `crm.email_message` first, then joins labels; the count RPC computes full
453	tab badges server-side.
454	
455	Future sessions should:
456	Keep Email Routing's table on `useEmailsQuery(EMAIL_ROUTE_LIMIT)` with a
457	positive limit, and keep tab badges on `useEmailSegmentCountsQuery`. Do not
458	restore `useEmailsQuery(-1)` for Email Routing or use full queue paging to
459	compute counts. Small explicit-limit global search against
460	`api.crm_email_routing_queue` is still acceptable.

564	### DataTable horizontal scroll can be caused by header resize handles
565	
566	What changed:
567	On 2026-06-30, Data Admin's department table showed a horizontal scrollbar even
568	when there was visible empty page space. Two causes overlapped: the page body was
569	capped at `max-w-6xl`, and `DataTable` rendered as `w-max` with resize handles
570	positioned `right-[-4px]`, making the last header cell overflow by 4px.
571	
572	Why:
573	The table was being squeezed inside an artificially narrow centered wrapper; the
574	off-cell resize handle then created a scrollbar even when the table otherwise
575	fit.
576	
577	Future sessions should:
578	Keep wide table pages full-width unless there is a deliberate readability reason
579	to cap them. If a table scrollbar looks unnecessary, verify with Playwright by
580	comparing the table wrapper's `clientWidth` and `scrollWidth`; do not trust the
581	visual screenshot alone. Current intended behavior is `DataTable` tables use
582	`min-w-full`, and resize handles stay inside header cells (`right-0`).
583	
584	## Credentials and environment
585	
586	No secret values appear here or in the repo.
587	
588	| Variable | Purpose | Stored where | Required in dev | Required in prod |
589	|---|---|---|---|---|
590	| `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL (build-time) | `.env` (dev); CI build arg/secret in prod | yes | yes |
591	| `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Supabase public anon key (build-time) | `.env` (dev); CI build arg/secret in prod | yes | yes |
592	| `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` | logo.dev **publishable** token (client-safe) for domain-derived customer logos | optional `.env` (dev); GitHub Actions secret `LOGODEV_TOKEN` → Docker build-arg (prod) | no (falls back to initials) | no (falls back to initials) |
593	| `COOLIFY_BASE_URL` | Coolify deploy API base for CI | GitHub Actions secret | n/a | n/a (CI only) |
594	| `COOLIFY_API_TOKEN` | Token to trigger Coolify deploy | GitHub Actions secret | n/a | n/a (CI only) |
595	| `COOLIFY_SERVER_UUID` | Coolify **application** uuid to deploy | GitHub Actions secret | n/a | n/a (CI only) |
596	| `LOGODEV_TOKEN` | logo.dev publishable token → `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` build-arg | GitHub Actions secret (optional) | n/a | n/a (CI only) |
597	| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GHCR push (built-in) | GitHub Actions (auto) | n/a | n/a (CI only) |
598	
599	Runtime auth is Supabase Auth (Azure OAuth/password). The SPA holds only the
600	public Supabase URL/anon key and the optional publishable logo.dev key; never put
601	service-role keys or real `.env` files in this repo.
602	
603	## Deployment
604	
605	Single orchestrated workflow: push to `main` → GitHub Actions builds, publishes,
606	and triggers Coolify. CI never SSHes into or mutates the server. Full detail in
607	`docs/deployment.md`; summary:
608	
609	- Workflow: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (name: **Build and Deploy**), jobs
610	  `verify` → `build-and-push` → `deploy`.
611	- Image/package: `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web`; tags `latest`, `main`, `sha-<commit-sha>`.
612	- Platform/app: Coolify app `popcrm-web` (`a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt`), project
613	  "POP Creations CRM" (`yp84tp0tmmshhcebgsd4j463`), env `production`.
614	- Deploy trigger: GitHub Actions `POST {COOLIFY_BASE_URL}/api/v1/deploy?uuid=<app>` (bearer token).
615	- Rollback: in Coolify, redeploy a previous `sha-<sha>` image (immutable tag).
616	- Runtime env: owned by Coolify (domains/ports/health/restart). App config is
617	  build-time `VITE_*` (static SPA), not Coolify runtime env.
618	- **SSH is not the normal path.** Manual `docker run` on the host is emergency/
619	  break-glass only; restore the Coolify-managed deploy immediately afterward.
620	- Branching: single-branch model — commit to `main`; do not create feature
621	  branches for this repo. Documentation / AI-context-only changes (`docs/**`,
622	  `**/*.md`, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, `.copilotignore`) skip the build.
623	
624	## Critical incidents
625	
626	### 2026-06-22 Live-query refactor capped CRM screens
627	
628	What happened:
629	After the TanStack Query refactor (`494b588`), primary CRM screens appeared to
630	lose most records. Customers loaded only the first 100 companies, Email Routing
631	only 50 messages, Pipeline/Programs only 100 opportunities, Notes only 50, Tasks
632	only 100, and related drawers/pickers/global search used the same partial slices.
633	
634	Impact:
635	Production data was intact, but the UI presented partial datasets as complete
636	lists and produced wrong tab counts, related-record counts, overview stats, and
637	search results.
638	
639	Root cause:
640	The refactor replaced global full loads with page-scoped query hooks but added
641	hard-coded client limits before server-side pagination/search/count contracts
642	existed. A bounded page query without an aggregate/count/search contract silently
643	lies in this CRM.
644	
645	Recovery:
646	`698885b` restored full paged reads (`limit = -1`) for main CRM record surfaces,
647	drawers, command search, and overview stats. Follow-up work made Contacts load by
648	server segment, Customers load eagerly, Customers Triage load from
649	`api.crm_ingested_domain_list`, and Not-a-Customer/All load only on demand.
650	
651	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
652	Do not add arbitrary positive limits to CRM list hooks unless the page also has
653	server-side pagination, search/filtering, and true aggregate counts. If a page
654	shows "All" or computes tab counts/client filters from a query, that query must
655	represent the full relevant dataset or a documented server segment with counts.
656	
657	### 2026-06-22 Contacts zero after Supabase cutover — contact view timeout/access gate
658	
659	What happened:
660	`/contacts` showed 0 for every segment after the Directus-to-Supabase import,
661	even though the Supabase tables contained 8,654 contacts and 3,744 canonical
662	companies.
663	
664	Impact:
665	The Contacts page looked empty for authenticated users and invited rerunning the
666	data import unnecessarily.
667	
668	Root cause:
669	Two issues overlapped: users without a linked `app.profile.auth_user_id` could
670	authenticate but see no CRM rows, and the original `security_invoker`
671	`api.crm_contact_list` view timed out on the browser's third 1,000-row page when
672	PostgREST applied derived-field ordering/filtering.
673	
674	Recovery:
675	The import was reconciled, affected profiles were linked to Supabase Auth users,
676	the frontend stopped server-side contact ordering/filtering, and migration
677	`20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql` preserved `api.crm_contact_list` as an
678	access-gated `security_invoker=false` view and added server-computed contact
679	segments/counts.
680	
681	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
682	Verify authenticated REST page loads (`0-999`, `1000-1999`, `2000-2999`, etc.)
683	and profile/app-access mapping before changing contact segmentation or rerunning
684	the Directus import.
685	
686	### 2026-06-22 Bad Gateway after deploy — Coolify proxy lost Docker socket
687	
688	What happened:
689	After a successful deploy, `crm.designflow.app` returned 502 while the app
690	container was healthy and nginx inside the container returned 200.
691	
692	Impact:
693	The live CRM was unreachable even though the new static bundle was running.
694	
695	Root cause:
696	`coolify-proxy`/Traefik logged `Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at
697	unix:///var/run/docker.sock`, so Traefik could not discover the newly deployed
698	Coolify container and had no valid upstream.
699	
700	Recovery:
701	Restarting `coolify-proxy` restored Docker provider discovery and the site
702	returned HTTP 200.
703	
704	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
705	When live returns 502 after deploy, first check `docker ps`, app container logs,
706	and `docker exec <container> wget http://127.0.0.1/contacts`. If the app is
707	healthy but proxy logs show Docker provider errors, `docker restart coolify-proxy`
708	restores route discovery.
709	
710	### 2026-06-12 Every page blank — approvals schema mismatch + all-or-nothing loader
711	
712	What happened:
713	After the redesign deploy, every page showed "Something went wrong / data could not be loaded."
714	
715	Impact:
716	The whole CRM UI was unusable while signed in (no page rendered data).
717	
718	Root cause:
719	`fetchApprovalThreads` requested `crm_licensor_approval_thread` fields that don't
720	exist in Directus (`licensor_name`, `approval_status`, `submitted_at`, `approved_at`,
721	`latest_comment`). Directus returned 403 for the unknown fields. The loader used
722	`Promise.all`, so that single rejection failed the entire bootstrap and blanked all pages.
723	
724	Recovery:
725	Two commits: `29ea195` switched the loader to `Promise.allSettled` (resilience);
726	`3592b88` mapped approvals to the real fields (`property_name`, `stage`,
727	`submitted_date`, `response_date`, `due_date`, `licensor_comments`). Verified the
728	corrected query returns 200 and the live site loads.
729	
730	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
731	Never load all collections all-or-nothing; verify requested fields against the real
732	Directus schema (`information_schema.columns`) before relying on them.
733	
734	### 2026-06-11 Migration: raw `docker run` → Coolify-managed deploy
735	
736	What happened:
737	The app was running as a hand-labeled raw `docker run` container with no CI; it was
738	migrated to the compliant GitHub Actions → GHCR → Coolify pipeline and cut over.
739	
740	Impact:
741	Brief, controlled cutover of `crm.designflow.app` (domains moved to the Coolify app,
742	old container removed). No data at risk (frontend stores none).
743	
744	Root cause:
745	N/A (planned migration to meet the CI/CD operating rules).
746	
747	Recovery:
748	N/A — verified live on the new pipeline; old container removed (image kept locally as a temporary artifact).
749	
750	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
751	Production deploys go through the workflow only; the old raw-container runbook is
752	now break-glass only (see `docs/deployment.md`).
753	
754	## Pending work
755	
756	| Status | Item | Owner/next action |
757	|---|---|---|
758	| done | Redesign, CI/CD pipeline, Coolify cutover, data-load fixes | Completed in commits up to `3592b88` |
759	| done | Full UI redesign: all pages, drawers, charts, tokens, board/list toggles | All screens complete; `frontend_imp.md` fully implemented |
760	| done | Tasks board view | Kanban columns (TODO/In progress/Done/Blocked) with TaskCard, drag-to-status via chip |
761	| done | Pipeline list/board toggle | DataTable list view + existing board; segmented control in ListBar |
762	| done | OpportunityModal: Ask AI, Share, Expand/collapse | All three top-bar actions wired; composer calls `createNote` API |
763	| done | `label()` enum overrides | `AI`, `DETERMINISTIC`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `TODO`, routing statuses all map to readable labels |
764	| done | Email routing: Method column | Replaced Department column; `MethodChip` + `MethodConfidence` in drawer |
765	| done | Overview activity panels | Reduced to 2 (Meetings + Approvals); deep-links via `useRecordSelection` |
766	| done | Approvals columns | Name · Licensor · Status · Submitted · Program · Latest comment per spec |
767	| done | All record drawers polished | Meeting/Task/Email/Note/Approval drawers match spec |
768	| done | DataTable ag-Grid-style tools | Persistent per-column filter icon → checkbox value popover; per-column header quick-search + autocomplete; visible resize separator; inline-edit dropdowns (`editOptions`/`onCellEdit`); spreadsheet drag-to-copy fill handle |
769	| done | Customers status/chain UX | Real-schema colored chips; inline-editable; Twenty logos via logo.dev; segmented tabs (Customers/Triage/Not a customer/All), with Triage backed by `api.crm_ingested_domain_list` |
770	| done | Customer status data normalized | 24 null `customer_status` rows → `UNASSIGNED` (direct Directus PG write); now one "New Company" bucket |
771	| done | Directus-to-Supabase CRM data import/reconciliation | Completed 2026-06-22; verified 8,654 contacts, 3,744 canonical customer rows, 38 departments, 11,267 emails, 27 meetings, and matching zero-count tables |
772	| done | Contacts zero after Supabase cutover | Fixed 2026-06-22 via frontend client-side contact segmentation plus `api.crm_contact_list` access-gated view migration |
773	| done | Live-query refactor cap regression | Fixed 2026-06-22; restored full reads/true counts for CRM screens and documented the "no arbitrary limits without server contracts" rule |
774	| blocked | Customer logos go live | Code deployed but inert until `LOGODEV_TOKEN` GitHub secret is set (publishable logo.dev key) — see HANDOFF.md. Falls back to initials until then |
775	| open | Server-side pagination / Supabase aggregates | Currently client-side for CRM screens; revisit if record volumes grow |
776	| open | Bump CI actions off Node 20 | GitHub deprecates Node-20 actions (~2026-06-16); update `actions/*` and `docker/*` versions in `deploy.yml` |
777	| known | Pre-existing lint warnings in `src/auth/auth.tsx` | 3 warnings (`any`, setState-in-effect, unused disable) accepted; do not add new warnings elsewhere |
778	| unknown | `crm_licensor_approval_thread.stage` values | Free-form, 0 rows today; verify real values via backend when approval data exists |
779	<!-- ansible-host-policy: managed rollout from u2giants/ansible -->
780	## Host / server changes — do NOT make them here
781	
782	The `hetz` server's host/OS layer is managed by **Ansible** in **[`u2giants/ansible`](https://github.com/u2giants/ansible)**.
783	To change the server (packages, users, firewall, DNS, Docker *engine* config, system cron,
784	systemd units, Cloudflare Tunnel 1, the backup watchdog), **open a PR there** and let CI apply
785	it — **never** SSH into the box and hand-edit it. Manual changes are drift and get reverted by
786	the next apply. See [`u2giants/ansible/AGENTS.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/ansible/blob/main/AGENTS.md).
787	
788	This repo is **not** the host layer. Its own changes belong here and deploy through their normal
789	pipeline (e.g. Coolify). Don't put host-level changes here, and don't manage this service's
790	container with Ansible. Scope boundary: **Ansible owns the host; Coolify owns the apps.**
791

# AGENTS.md — popcrm-web

Canonical operating guide and documentation router for **popcrm-web**. Read this
first; load other docs only when the task needs them (see the Documentation map).

## Project summary

`popcrm-web` is the **POP CRM frontend** — a Vite/React/TypeScript single-page app
served by nginx, used by internal POP Creations staff to run customer, sales,
licensing and product-development workflows: customers, contacts, the program
pipeline, Outlook-ingested email routing, Fireflies meeting notes, notes, tasks,
licensor approvals, and AI-model settings.

It **stores no data of its own** — every read/write goes through the shared
Supabase project (`https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`). The CRM backend is
a Supabase/Postgres port of the earlier Twenty→Directus CRM data model. The
outcome that matters: a fast, dense operations console over that shared data.

- Production: `https://crm.designflow.app`
- Preview alias: `https://crm-dev.designflow.app`
- Backend (Supabase): `https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`
- Fireflies webhook/health: `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app`
- Sibling frontends (separate repos): `poppim-web` (PIM), `popdam-web` (DAM)

## Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.

## Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with:

- `AGENTS.md`

Then load additional docs only when relevant:

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/`, `frontend_imp.md` |
| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow/shape changes | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
| Add/change config, env vars, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
| Change local setup, dev/test/lint scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, `package.json`, `eslint.config.js` | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release, or rollback | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf` | Local-only dev docs |
| Change data shape, Supabase fields, queries, views, RPCs, or identifiers | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/database.types.ts`, `src/features/crm/api.ts`, `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` | Deployment docs unless rollout changes |
| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md` (Critical incidents), area-relevant source, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Unrelated docs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md` (if present) and the docs it names | Docs outside the handoff scope |
| Understand the redesign rationale (charts, tokens, layout) | `frontend_imp.md` (historical plan — fully implemented) | Everything else |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless the task requires them |
| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/`, ignore files | Source files except to verify accuracy |
| Pull secrets from 1Password (MCP server or `op` CLI) | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/1password.md` | Unrelated architecture/deploy docs |

Notes:
- `HANDOFF.md` is **absent** when there is no unfinished work. If it exists, it is
  required reading for continuation tasks.
- `frontend_imp.md` is a large background design plan — now fully implemented. It is in
  `.claudeignore`/`.cursorignore` to keep it out of routine AI context.
- `design_handoff_popcrm_elevation/` is a historical design-spec directory — also ignored.
- Shared non-code infrastructure/server standards live in
  [`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
  When deployment, domains, runtime ownership, server dependencies, break-glass
  runbooks, or infrastructure decisions change for this app, update that repo too.

## Shared-backend startup/shutdown hygiene

Why this exists:
`popcrm-web`, `poppim-web`, and `popdam3` all depend on the same Supabase backend.
An unfinished migration or dirty canonical `u2giants/shared-db` checkout can block
unrelated app commits or, worse, ship a database change without the right preview
checks. Future AI sessions must keep shared-db work isolated and leave the
workspace clean enough for the next vibe-coding session.

Startup checklist:

1. Run `git status --short` in this repo before editing.
2. If the task may touch Supabase schema, RLS, API views/RPCs, generated database
   types, or cross-app data contracts, also run `git status --short` in
   `/worksp/shared-db` before editing.
3. Treat `shared-db/` inside this repo as a read-only mirror. Do not create or
   edit migrations there; use canonical `/worksp/shared-db`.
4. If `/worksp/shared-db` has untracked migrations or unrelated dirty files, stop
   and report them before creating new database work. Do not mix another
   session's shared-db changes into this app's commit.
5. Before creating a shared-db migration, create/switch to a dedicated
   `/worksp/shared-db` branch named for the database change. App repos commit to
   `main`; shared-db uses branch + PR.

Shutdown checklist:

1. Run `git status --short` in this repo and, if touched or inspected for backend
   work, in `/worksp/shared-db`.
2. No untracked shared-db migration may remain. Every shared-db migration must be
   committed on its own branch, stashed with a clear name, or removed if
   abandoned.
3. If shared-db work is incomplete, leave durable handoff text that names the
   branch/stash, migration file, preview/prod apply status, and the next exact
   action.
4. Final reports must separate app commits from shared-db status so the owner can
   keep vibe-coding without becoming the git janitor.

## Repository structure

Project-owned application code:

- `src/app/` — shell + router: `AppLayout.tsx`, `routes.tsx`, `navigation.ts`
- `src/components/app/` — shared building blocks: `DataTable`, `DetailDrawer`,
  `MetricCard`, `PageToolbar`, `AppPage`, `AppSidebar`, `AppHeader`, `Combobox`,
  `CommandSearch`, `FilterSelect`, `StatusBadge`, `states.tsx`
- `src/features/crm/` — CRM domain:
  - `CrmDataContext.tsx` — loads all collections once; exposes state, refresh, stats
  - `api.ts` — Supabase view/RPC/table reads and writes · `constants.ts` · `format.ts` · `useRecordSelection.ts`
  - `pages/` — one module per route (Overview, Pipeline, Customers, Contacts,
    EmailRouting, Meetings, Notes, Tasks, Approvals, Settings) + `_shared.ts`
  - `components/` — domain drawers (Email/Opportunity/Customer/Contact/Task/Note/
    Meeting/Approval) + `CrmStatusBadge`, `RelationLabel`
- `src/auth/auth.tsx` — Supabase auth/profile state · `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`
- `src/lib/` — `supabase.ts` (client), `database.types.ts` (generated schema), `types.ts` (frontend domain types), `utils.ts`
- `src/App.tsx`, `src/main.tsx`, `src/index.css` (shared OKLCH design tokens)

Generated-style primitives (shadcn): `src/components/ui/*` — see Core modification inventory.

Build / config / deploy:

- `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `components.json`, `package.json`

Docs: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/*`, `frontend_imp.md`

Build artifacts / ignored: `dist/`, `node_modules/` (see What to ignore).

Shared Supabase migrations live under `shared-db/supabase/migrations/`; legacy
Directus code under `/worksp/directus` is rollback/reference context only.

## Prime Directive: custom-code boundary

Our custom code lives here:

- `src/app/`
- `src/components/app/`
- `src/features/`
- `src/auth/`, `src/pages/`, `src/lib/`
- `docs/`
- `.github/workflows/`
- root config: `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `package.json`

Everything else requires justification before touching. In particular,
`src/components/ui/*` are shadcn-style primitives (see below).

## Core modification inventory

Files modified outside the clearly project-owned application areas:

| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/components/ui/*.tsx` | Hand-authored shadcn-style primitives (tabs, select, table, tooltip, popover, dialog, label, chart, etc.) using the unified `radix-ui` import style | shadcn CLI/registry not run here; primitives added to match existing ones | Running `npx shadcn add` could overwrite/conflict; keep the `radix-ui` (not `@radix-ui/*`) import style and Lucide icons |
| `Dockerfile` | Added `COMMIT_HASH` / `COMMIT_DATE` / `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` build args before `npm run build` | `.git` is dockerignored (commit identity passed by CI); `VITE_*` is build-time so the logo.dev token must be baked at build | If the header build-stamp is removed, drop the commit args too; `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` is optional (empty → initials avatars) |

No third-party/vendor/framework source files are modified. `src/components/ui` is
the only "generated-style" area, and it is hand-maintained in this repo.

## Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes

| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
|---|---|---|
| Change a CRM page/table/filter | `src/features/crm/pages/<Page>.tsx` | `src/components/ui/*` |
| Change a record drawer | `src/features/crm/components/<X>Drawer.tsx` | unrelated pages |
| Add/adjust a Supabase query, field, view, or RPC | `src/features/crm/api.ts`, `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/database.types.ts`, `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` | legacy Directus schema unless explicitly doing rollback/reference work |
| Add a route / nav item | `src/app/routes.tsx`, `src/app/navigation.ts` | shell internals unless needed |
| Change status colors / stage tones | `src/features/crm/constants.ts`, `src/index.css` (tokens) | per-component ad-hoc colors |
| Add a shared UI building block | `src/components/app/` | `src/components/ui/*` (primitives only) |
| Change a base primitive | `src/components/ui/<name>.tsx` | — (note in Core modification inventory) |
| Change build/deploy | `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf` | the production server directly |
| Add an env/config value | `src/lib/supabase.ts`, `docs/configuration.md`; CI build args for `VITE_*` | a real `.env` in the repo |

## Data model and external identifiers

Supabase CRM entities this app reads/writes. Browser reads generally go through
`api.crm_*` views; guarded core writes use `api.crm_update_*` RPCs; CRM-owned row
writes use `crm.*` tables. Frontend domain types are in `src/lib/types.ts`;
generated database types are in `src/lib/database.types.ts`:

| Entity/System | Identifier | Where defined | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Customer | `core.customer` / `api.customer_list` (shared) plus CRM-specific `api.crm_customer_*` contracts | Supabase | shared customer hub. `is_potential = false` means PLM/ERP-confirmed active customer; `true` means tracked potential customer. `customer_status` remains the CRM workflow/status axis. Use `api.crm_customer_segment_list` / `api.crm_customer_segment_counts` for CRM customer page tabs and active pickers. Legacy `api.crm_account_list` / `api.crm_update_account` names are deprecated compatibility contracts only; do not add new callers. `api.crm_customer_list.logo_url` exposes PLM-imported full-width logo URLs when available |
| Ingested domain | `crm.ingested_domain` / `api.crm_ingested_domain_list` | Supabase | CRM-only email-domain triage inbox. These rows are **not** customers and are not shared with PM/DAM/PLM. Promote with `crm.promote_ingested_domain(...)` to create a potential `core.customer` row |
